_readme: g2-import-v2.2 judge-pB upgrade (Haiku-4.5 -> Opus-4.8) assumption block ASM-1870..1879 — EMITTED by the Fable BUILD agent (2026-07-12, second v2.2 build pass, maintainer directive issue #25) for central registration by the coordinator at successor-record freeze; registry/assumptions.jsonl is NOT touched by this build (same discipline as the 1820..1828 block).
Range 1870-1879 verified free against registry/assumptions.jsonl at emission time (max registered id 1867; repo-wide grep for ASM-187x empty); ids 1870-1879 used.

Scope: fold pB = claude-opus-4-8 (judge id judge-pB-opus48, flag --pb-model opus, v2.2-scoped) into the g2-import-v2.2 successor design, shipping TOGETHER with the v2.2 composite-hedge rubric fix (asm-v22-1820-1828.json). Design record: docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md sections 2b, 3-6. $0 build: mock-only, zero judge calls, NO freeze, NO git actions, NO pilot launch (coordinator action).

Rider carried by every forward-looking row, verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.
Tags: MEASURED = read from committed bytes/run files this tick; STIPULATED = design or maintainer decision, rationale given; EXTRAPOLATION = forward claim, resolution_path given.

rows.claim: Channel attribution for the sanctioned pilot's 11 A3 disagreements (runs/real-20260712-auth, AC1_A3 0.6222, table 28/1/7/4): 7 of 11 are the judge-pB (Haiku-4.5) hedge-scope channel — pB reading hedged multi-clause composites at per-part/unhedged strength (g2:pi:016, 021, 025, 033, 064, 068, 089) — and the independent Fable third-read sided against pB on all 7 (016/033/064/089 'sound'; 021/025/068 'sound (lean)'). The remaining 4 (g2:pi:036, 037, 070, 071) are the judge-pA (GPT-5.6-Sol) verb-sense channel: bare-parenthetical polysemous verbs (find x2, make x2) where pA admits non-central senses into the ALL-cases quantifier; on 071 pA's 'no' contradicts its own 'yes' on schema-identical birth/death items — the one clean panel datum. Counterfactually converting the 7 pB-channel items gives AC1 ~ 0.88 at pilot marginals.
rows.rationale: Grounds the maintainer's judge-replacement directive in the item-level panel-vs-assessment analysis; separates the channel the pB upgrade + hedge bullet address (7) from the channel they do not (4, ASM-1877).
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/analysis/g2-panel-vs-assessment.md (40-row table + section 'Implication for the next step'); poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/runs/real-20260712-auth/judge-p{A,B}-a3-pilot-responses.jsonl

rows.claim: Maintainer directive (issue #25): the g2-import-v2.2 SUCCESSOR's judge-pB is claude-opus-4-8 (judge id judge-pB-opus48), replacing claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, via the SAME headless-claude machinery, pinned system prompt, template, seeds, and gates. The v2.2 pair is pA = GPT-5.6-Sol + pB = Opus-4.8, and the hedge-scope rubric fix (ASM-1822/1823) and the judge upgrade ship TOGETHER: the one funded Stage-P pilot tests the combined instrument. The FROZEN g2-import-v2 record's pB = Haiku-4.5 pin is untouched — Opus exists only behind --pb-model opus, which requires --rubric v22. Disclosed cost of bundling: a v2.2 pilot PASS cannot attribute credit between rubric fix and judge swap; the maintainer accepts this because the pre-commitment (ASM-1876) bounds the family to this one iteration either way. Vendor-family overlap disclosure carries verbatim (Opus-4.8 is the same vendor family as Haiku-4.5 and the Fable design/assessment agents; pB remains the SENSITIVITY judge, never sole gold).
rows.rationale: Maintainer decision, recorded with its evidential basis (ASM-1870: 7/11 disagreements are the pB channel and the third-read sided against pB on all 7) and its disclosed attribution cost; not a measured claim about Opus's judging behaviour.

rows.claim: Build pass 2026-07-12 (second v2.2 pass), $0, zero judge calls: run-ontg2v2.py extended with the flag-guarded --pb-model opus path — activate_pb_model dies (ERR_ONTG2V2_PBMODEL) unless RUBRIC == 'v22' (parsed after --rubric regardless of argv order); JUDGE_CFG['pB'] becomes {id: judge-pB-opus48, kind: claude, model: claude-opus-4-8}; pb_model is stamped into preflight-status.json and pilot-status.json with fail-closed mix guards in _require_preflight and _require_pilot_pass; validate_claude gains an OPUS_MODEL branch (ASM-1873); _proxy_out_base and phase_assemble route ALL opus-pB response files into run_dir (pA-proxy discipline) so canonical BASE locations stay reserved for the frozen record. New sha 16d71d8ffbaa09d0ec1dcd86770edf97a555b00571ca2b6e7b581637dda77c5b — to be pinned by the successor record with both judge CLI banners at its ops amendment. Frozen-path intactness: all 25 frozen-record pins.artifact_hashes re-verified byte-identical this tick EXCEPT run-ontg2v2.py, whose drift ce2ab5a6 -> cd8e2f89 -> efe134ad -> 16d71d8f was already disclosed (ASM-1824/1828) and is lawfully absorbed at the successor freeze; the flagless invocation preserves pB = claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with the exact-single-key modelUsage identity check unchanged.
rows.rationale: Records exactly what changed on disk and that nothing the frozen record pins moved (beyond the pre-disclosed harness drift); the frozen pB path's guard is structural (flag + rubric scope + mix guards), not conventional.
rows.backing_ref: poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/run-ontg2v2.py (activate_pb_model, validate_claude, _proxy_out_base, __main__ flag parsing); registry/experiments/g2-import-v2.json pins.artifact_hashes; docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md sections 2b + 3

rows.claim: modelUsage identity allowance for opus-pB: the headless claude CLI is expected to spawn the same background haiku helper turn observed under the fable pA proxy (2026-07-12 probe: init.model = requested model, modelUsage = {requested, haiku}), so validate_claude accepts, FOR model == claude-opus-4-8 ONLY, modelUsage key sets satisfying (claude-opus-4-8 present) AND (keys subset of {claude-opus-4-8, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001}). This is extrapolated from the fable observation, not measured for opus — no opus call was made this build ($0). The check remains fail-closed: init.model must equal claude-opus-4-8, apiKeySource must be 'none', and any other key set aborts the run (ERR_ONTG2V2_IDENTITY). The frozen pB=Haiku exact-single-key check is byte-unchanged.
rows.rationale: The helper turn is a CLI property, not a model property, on the one probe available; encoding the allowance now avoids a guaranteed first-call abort if the helper appears, while the fail-closed check guarantees no silent mis-identity either way.
rows.resolution_path: The first live v2.2 preflight pB call (coordinator's pilot launch) surfaces the actual modelUsage keys in its events.jsonl. If they violate the allowance the run aborts fail-closed at preflight before any real item is judged; the successor record's ops amendment then records the observed key set and any needed one-line allowance correction as an ops (non-constitutive) fix.
rows.backing_ref: poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/run-ontg2v2.py (OPUS_MU_ALLOWED, validate_claude; FABLE_MU_ALLOWED comment for the fable probe observation)

rows.claim: Budget under pB=Opus: call counts are unchanged (pilot stage 132, full-campaign ceiling 796; the flag changes WHICH model answers pB, not how often), and the pinned conservative $0.012/call dollar bound still covers the opus half. Measured prompt envelope this tick: max assembled prompt + system prompt over all 282 instrument items = 3,126 bytes (mean 2,916) ~ <= 900 input tokens at a conservative 3.5 bytes/token; at Opus-4.8 API pricing ($5/$25 per MTok) that is ~$0.0045 input + ~$0.0005 for the contract-conformant ~20-token JSON answer ~= $0.005/call point estimate; even a pathological 300-token output lands at ~$0.012, i.e. AT the pinned bound. Additionally pB calls draw NO API dollars at all: the headless claude path strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN and the identity check REQUIRES apiKeySource == 'none' (OAuth subscription), so the bound-implied ledger remains a conservative accounting device, and the $10 usd_cap arithmetic (796 x $0.012 = $9.552) is unchanged. Known pre-existing looseness, disclosed not introduced: _budget_check counts items, not content-retry attempts (frozen semantics, <= 3 attempts/item).
rows.rationale: Sizes the opus swap against the frozen review-fix-5 dollar-abort machinery without touching it; the pinned bound was derived from the haiku+codex ledger, so its coverage of opus is arithmetic on this tick's measured prompt bytes plus published pricing, not a measurement of opus calls.
rows.resolution_path: The v2.2 pilot's per-call token usage is read back from the pB events.jsonl result records (modelUsage token counts) at pilot completion; if any call's API-equivalent cost exceeds $0.012 the successor's ops amendment re-derives the bound (and the ceiling arithmetic) before the full 796-call run is authorized.
rows.backing_ref: poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/run-ontg2v2.py (_budget_check, PRICE_BOUND_USD_PER_CALL, run_claude env stripping, validate_claude apiKeySource check); docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md section 6 (pilot cost)

rows.claim: The calibration/preflight anchors are model-agnostic: the 10 preflight anchors (2 v1 items cal:g2-1/2 + 8 hedge items cal:hedge-1..8) and the 8-item pilot cal set are plain concept/statement items with pre-keyed expected answers; a grep over calibration-items.jsonl, calibration-hedge-v22.jsonl, prompt-template-v2.2.txt, and the pinned pB system prompt finds ZERO model, vendor, or judge-name tokens (the only 'judge' hits are the generic role word in the template's first line and hedge bullet). Opus therefore faces byte-identical instrument inputs to Haiku — same pinned system prompt (sha be919831...), same template, same {{ITEM}} assembly — and must clear the SAME mechanical gates: 10/10 preflight anchors per judge (preflight dies on any miss) and 16/16 pilot cal across judges (PILOT_CAL_MIN = 16 under --rubric v22). No anchor, expected label, seed, or gate was edited for the judge swap.
rows.rationale: Confirms the directive's precondition that the known-answer channels transfer to the new judge unmodified: anchor validity is a property of the item text + ordinary meaning, enforced on Opus by the same fail-closed gates that bound Haiku.

rows.claim: PRE-COMMITMENT restated for the v2.2 pair (supersedes ASM-1825's pair-naming; recorded BEFORE any v2.2 judge call): if the v2.2 Stage-P pilot's Gwet AC1_A3 on the 40 pinned items is < 0.65 — a SECOND sanctioned AC1 pilot failure for this record family — the v2.2 proxy pair (GPT-5.6-Sol + Opus-4.8) is RETIRED as an adoption-arm (A3) instrument for the g2-import family AND no further LLM proxy pair may be substituted or funded for A3 in this family: the judge upgrade does NOT reset the one-iteration bound, closing the judge-substitution loophole a literal reading of ASM-1825 (which named the Haiku pair) would have opened. Haiku-4.5's pB role is retired NOW by maintainer directive (issue #25) rather than by a second failure. On fail, adoption authority passes to the two-human adjudicated panel on the 84 slots (the envelope's sole authority for permanent adoption). Retirement binds adoption-arm authority only: no conclusion about H-SOFT/H-SRC/H-SEP in either direction, no retirement for unhedged arms or other families, no binding of the human panel. A PASS licenses exactly the pre-registered ~$5-6 full 796-call run under the successor record, nothing else. Rider verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.
rows.rationale: Carries the instrument assessment's anti-shopping closeability call forward STRICTLY STRENGTHENED across the judge swap; either pilot branch still terminates the proxy iteration loop.
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md section 4 (restated blockquote + note vs ASM-1825); docs/next/analysis/correctness-track-instrument-assessment.md sections 2.1 and 4 item 2

rows.claim: The pA verb-sense channel is untouched by BOTH v2.2 changes and is expected to recur in the v2.2 pilot: the 4 residual disagreements (g2:pi:036/037 find, 070/071 make) are pA admitting non-central senses under BARE parentheticals, orthogonal to the hedge-scope bullet and to the pB model; the channel was deterministic across three same-day runs (pA flipped 2/120 answers overall, none on 036/037/070; the panel analysis calls the recurrence expected). Per issue #25 the render should ALSO pin verb sense where the parenthetical is bare — make 'as described above' point at an actual sense description (e.g. 'find (X finds Y — comes upon or locates Y)') instead of the bare '(X finds Y)'. NOT implemented this build: re-rendered A3 items conflict with the successor's verbatim-inheritance clause (byte-identical arm renderings; the SAME 40 pinned pilot items under the AC1 gate) and need their own estimand-identity + gold pass. OPEN pre-freeze coordinator/maintainer decision: (a) fund the sense-pinning render build BEFORE the pilot (consciously re-basing the 'same 40 items' pin), or (b) run the pilot with the disclosed ~4-item deterministic residual — viable, since the 7-item pB channel alone straddles the gate (counterfactual AC1 ~ 0.88), but it spends the family's LAST iteration with a known residual.
rows.rationale: Surfaces, without deciding, the one issue-#25 element this build cannot lawfully implement ($0, no frozen-material regeneration); prevents the residual from being discovered mid-pilot as if it were news.
rows.resolution_path: Coordinator/maintainer decision (a)-vs-(b) recorded at successor-record freeze; then the v2.2 pilot's item-level diff on g2:pi:036/037/070/071 measures whether the channel recurred (option b) or was closed (option a). Under option (b), 4/40 recurring splits at pilot-#2 marginals still permit AC1 >= 0.65 iff the pB channel is closed.
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/analysis/g2-panel-vs-assessment.md (pA channel + 'Implication for the next step'); docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md section 2b (options a/b); issue #25

rows.claim: Mechanism recommendation (unchanged from ASM-1826, now with pB=Opus folded in): v2.2 ships as SUCCESSOR record registry/experiments/g2-import-v2.2.json (kot-reg/2), NOT an amendment of the frozen g2-import-v2. It inherits the estimand identity chain and ALL v2 gates verbatim (pilot AC1_A3 >= 0.65 on the SAME 40 pinned items, decisive >= 36/40, hedge-flip <= 2/8, full-run gates, judge-pA protocol), quarantines both prior pilots as instrument evidence, and pins: judge-pB = judge-pB-opus48 / claude-opus-4-8 (the ONE inherited-protocol departure, maintainer-directed), prompt-template-v2.2.txt 60d34037..., calibration-hedge-v22.jsonl 63c247a9..., run-ontg2v2.py 16d71d8f..., invocation verbatim '--rubric v22 --pb-model opus', cal gates 10/10 preflight per judge / 16/16 pilot, call ceiling 796, the ASM-1876 pre-commitment as a verdict-bearing clause, and ASM blocks 1820..1828 + 1870..1879 registered at freeze. The ASM-1877 render decision (option a or b) must be recorded in the successor before freeze. NOTHING is frozen by this build; freeze, reuse-check, and the pilot launch are coordinator actions.
rows.rationale: Same grounds as ASM-1826 (constitutive change; fired Stage-P gate; the record's own exit prescription; v1->v2 precedent), which the judge swap only strengthens — a judge model is as constitutive as a rubric.

rows.claim: Validation pass 2026-07-12, $0 (mock only, zero judge calls): (i) all five mock verdict paths GREEN under '--rubric v22 --pb-model opus' (runs/mockcheck-v22opus-20260712: go PASS / nogo FAIL / instrument INSTRUMENT-INVALID / pilotfail INSTRUMENT-INVALID with pilot_valid=false / breadth FAIL with breadth_confound=true; pilot-status records rubric=v22, pb_model=opus, cal 16/16), including flag-order independence (--pb-model before --rubric, same GREEN); (ii) default-path regression GREEN after the edit (runs/mockcheck-v2default-opusbuild-20260712: go + pilotfail flagless -> rubric=v2, pb_model=null, cal 12/12); (iii) guards exercised directly: --pb-model opus without --rubric v22 dies ERR_ONTG2V2_PBMODEL; --pb-model haiku dies; the pb-model mix guard fires in BOTH directions (v22-no-opus pilot-status vs v22+opus invocation, and the reverse); (iv) verify_pins green over the v22 pin set and all 25 frozen-record artifact_hashes byte-identical except the pre-disclosed run-ontg2v2.py drift; (v) anchor model-agnosticism grep clean (ASM-1875). Not exercised at $0, by design: the live opus identity path (ASM-1873) and per-call cost (ASM-1874) — both fail closed at the coordinator's preflight before any real item is judged.
rows.rationale: Green-mock-before-freeze discipline for the second build pass; separates what mock mechanics can prove ($0) from the two live-call extrapolations, each of which resolves at preflight, not mid-run.
